subject: [PATCH v5 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

reviews.author: Dave Jiang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Jiang applied the patch to a specific branch (for-7.1/cxl-region-refactor) and pushed it to cxl.git for 0-day coverage, but will wait until the end to merge due to potential merge conflicts.
reviews.raw_body:
I applied to for-7.1/cxl-region-refactor branch and pushed to the cxl.git so it gets 0-day coverage. But I'm going hold off until the end to merge this in case of merge conflicts.

29990ab5cb40 cxl/core: use cleanup.h for devm_cxl_add_dax_region
d747cf98f091 cxl/core/region: move dax region device logic into region_dax.c
8a1ec5fb2360 cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c

2026-03-27.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three separate files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. This change makes it clear that the logic applies to specific types of regions, reduces development conflicts, and improves code organization.

reviews.author: Anisa Su
reviews.summary: Reviewer Anisa Su noted that the patch is missing a crucial assignment to cxlr->cxlr_dax, which causes a segmentation fault when not present.
reviews.raw_body:
cxlr->cxlr_dax = cxlr_dax;

Running into segfaults without this ^

reviews.author: Dave Jiang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Jiang requested a separate 'fixes' patch to address any remaining issues, but did not specify the nature of these issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you pls create a fixes patch?

DJ

2026-04-10.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three separate files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. This change makes it clear that the logic applies to specific types of regions, reduces development conflicts, and improves code organization.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch is supposed to be a non-functional change, implying that the issue of functionality being lost during refactoring should not have occurred.
reviews.raw_body:
This should have been a purely non-functional change (the code is just
moving from A to B without changes).

That would mean the original code should run into this too unless I
mucked up the move.

~Gregory
